name: Coffee Cream Puffs
instructions:
1. For the craquelin topping, pulse the softened butter, brown sugar, and salt in a food processor until creamy and well combined. Add the flour and pulse just until the mixture comes together into a dough, being careful not to overmix. Drizzle in the vanilla extract and pulse once more. Turn out the dough onto a sheet of parchment paper, place another sheet on top, and roll it out to about 2mm thickness. Transfer to the freezer for at least 30 minutes until firm, then use a round cutter to cut circles slightly larger than your piped choux mounds.

2. For the milk tea pastry cream, bring the milk to a gentle simmer in a saucepan. Remove from heat, add the black tea leaves or tea bag, cover, and steep for 5-7 minutes to infuse the flavor. Strain out the tea leaves. In a separate bowl, whisk together the sugar, cornstarch, salt, and egg yolks until smooth and pale. Slowly pour the warm tea-infused milk into the yolk mixture while whisking constantly to temper the eggs. Return the mixture to the saucepan and cook over medium heat, whisking continuously, until it thickens into a pudding-like consistency, about 3-5 minutes. Remove from heat, whisk in the butter until melted and glossy, then transfer to a bowl, press plastic wrap directly onto the surface to prevent a skin from forming, and refrigerate until completely chilled.

3. For the coffee pastry cream, bring the milk to a simmer and whisk in the instant coffee until fully dissolved. In a bowl, whisk together the sugar, cornstarch, salt, and egg yolks until smooth. Temper the yolks by slowly adding the hot coffee milk while whisking constantly. Return to the saucepan and cook over medium heat, whisking continuously, until thick and glossy, about 3-5 minutes. Remove from heat, whisk in the butter, transfer to a bowl, cover with plastic wrap touching the surface, and chill thoroughly.

4. For the choux pastry, preheat your oven to 190°C (375°F) and line baking sheets with parchment. In a small bowl, whisk together the whole egg and egg white until combined—this will be your egg wash. In a medium saucepan, combine the milk, water, butter, sugar, and salt. Bring to a rolling boil over medium-high heat, ensuring the butter is fully melted. Remove from heat and immediately add all the flour at once, stirring vigorously with a wooden spoon until a smooth dough forms. Return to medium heat and cook, stirring constantly, for 1-2 minutes to dry out the dough—you'll see a thin film form on the bottom of the pan. Transfer the dough to a bowl and let it cool for 2-3 minutes. Gradually beat in the egg mixture, a little at a time, mixing thoroughly after each addition until the dough is smooth, glossy, and falls from the spoon in a thick ribbon. Transfer to a piping bag fitted with a large round tip and pipe mounds onto the prepared baking sheets, spacing them well apart.

5. Place a frozen craquelin round on top of each piped choux mound—the craquelin will create a beautiful crackled top. Bake for 25-30 minutes until the puffs are deeply golden and feel light and hollow. Don't open the oven door during baking or they may collapse. Once baked, immediately poke a small hole in the bottom of each puff with a skewer to release steam, which prevents sogginess. Let them cool completely on a wire rack.

6. To stabilize the whipped cream, bloom the gelatin in cold water for 5 minutes, then gently heat until dissolved. Whip the heavy cream with the powdered sugar until soft peaks form, then drizzle in the dissolved gelatin while continuing to whip until stiff peaks form. Gently fold half of the stabilized whipped cream into each of the chilled pastry creams until smooth and airy.

7. Fill a piping bag with each cream mixture. Cut each cooled puff in half horizontally or pipe the cream through the bottom hole. Fill half the puffs with milk tea cream and half with coffee cream. Dust with powdered sugar if desired and serve immediately for the best texture, or refrigerate for up to 2 hours.
simpleInstructions:
1. Blend craquelin ingredients, roll thin, freeze, and cut circles.

2. Steep tea in milk, make pastry cream with yolks and cornstarch, then chill.

3. Make coffee pastry cream the same way, then chill.

4. Boil milk mixture, stir in flour, cook to dry, beat in eggs, and pipe mounds.

5. Top with craquelin, bake until golden, poke holes, and cool.

6. Whip cream with gelatin, fold into pastry creams, and fill puffs.
